claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch normalizes provider-specific configuration to prevent drift between Claude, Codex, and custom providers, preserving tool capability under harness boundary.

evidence.summary: executionConfig() normalizes provider/executor, model, and tool profile with provider-specific branches that reset inherited state.
evidence.why: Shows provider configuration isolation and normalization logic preventing cross-provider leakage.

evidence.summary: Claude provider branch clears settings/tools flags, sets bypassPermissions, normalizes tool profile to boundary/research/full, and stores agent_tool_profile.
evidence.why: Demonstrates Claude-specific configuration isolation and tool profile normalization.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation() has separate code paths for codex (lines 43-61), custom (63-75), and claude (77-108) with no shared state.
evidence.why: Proves provider invocation paths are isolated with separate argument construction.

evidence.summary: execution_policy defines boundary: project_mutation=forbidden, allowed_write_roots=[artifact output dir], agent_tool_profile configuration.
evidence.why: Documents harness boundary constraints enforced during evidence dispatch.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() walks project files excluding .git/node_modules/dist, computes SHA-256 digests, and ignores allowed_write_roots.
evidence.why: Shows boundary violation detection mechanism via file digest comparison.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime() captures before/after boundary snapshots, runs agents, diffs changes, and injects BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers.
evidence.why: Proves runtime boundary enforcement and blocker injection for violations.

evidence.summary: test 'codex dispatch does not inherit a claude default model or force read-only sandbox' asserts provider isolation.
evidence.why: Validates codex provider configuration is isolated from Claude defaults.

evidence.summary: test 'claude dispatch does not pass tool restriction flags and relies on boundary enforcement' confirms tool capability preservation.
evidence.why: Verifies Claude evidence mode preserves full tool capability under boundary.

evidence.summary: test 'claude full tool profile requires explicit authorization for full semantic mode' validates full profile guard.
evidence.why: Proves full tool profile requires explicit allowFullAgentTools flag.

evidence.summary: test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' confirms boundary enforcement.
evidence.why: Validates boundary violation detection and blocker injection.

evidence.summary: buildAgentAdapterCapabilities() returns provider-specific capability objects (codex/custom/claude) with isolated sandbox/approval/file_write/shell_exec fields.
evidence.why: Demonstrates provider capability contracts are isolated.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer/cross-checker/verifier protocols with writes_code=false and result_schema.
evidence.why: Documents evidence agent protocols forbid writes and specify schema.

risks: A provider-specific configuration assumption could silently weaken dispatch if executionConfig() normalization logic misses a branch.
Boundary snapshot may miss mutations if git ls-files fails or file walk excludes untracked files outside ignored patterns.
Custom provider sandbox behavior remains caller-owned and is not validated by YOLO contract.
missing: No evidence of real-world stress testing with actual Claude -p agents beyond unit test mocks.
No documentation or test coverage for race conditions between snapshot capture and agent execution.
No evidence that provider-specific flags like codex_sandbox/approval are validated for safe values.

result.notes: Evidence shows provider configuration is normalized per-provider with isolated code paths, boundary enforcement detects project mutations, and tests cover key isolation scenarios. Missing stress testing with real agents is a verification gap, not a design flaw.
